task_completion.description: Open the http.cap file in Wireshark
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark application is open and displaying the contents of 'http.cap', visible in the title bar or recent files.

task_completion.description: Locate the first HTTP GET request packet
task_completion.visual_evidence: Packet number 4 is selected in the packet list, or a display filter like 'http.request.method == "GET"' is applied and the first result is selected.

task_completion.description: Add the required comment to the first HTTP GET request
task_completion.visual_evidence: A packet comment dialog for packet 4 is visible containing the exact text 'Initial HTTP GET request to server', or the packet details pane shows this comment for packet 4.

task_completion.description: Locate the first HTTP 200 OK response packet
task_completion.visual_evidence: Packet number 27 is selected in the packet list, or a display filter like 'http.response.code == 200' is applied and the first result is selected.

task_completion.description: Add the required comment to the first HTTP 200 OK response
task_completion.visual_evidence: A packet comment dialog for packet 27 is visible containing the exact text 'Server responded with 200 OK', or the packet details pane shows this comment for packet 27.

task_completion.description: Save the annotated capture as a pcapng file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible with the filename 'http_annotated.pcapng' and the format set to pcapng (Wireshark/tcpdump/... - pcapng), or a terminal/file manager shows the newly created file at /home/ga/Documents/captures/http_annotated.pcapng.

integrity.description: The agent used the Wireshark GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Wireshark graphical interface (menus, dialogs, packet lists) rather than using command-line tools like editcap or tshark to manipulate the file.

integrity.description: The agent saved all 43 original packets
integrity.visual_evidence: The Wireshark status bar shows 'Packets: 43' and the 'Save As' dialog does not have 'Displayed' or 'Selected' packets radio buttons checked in a way that would drop packets (it should save 'All packets').

integrity.description: The agent genuinely added packet comments via the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The comments were entered into the actual Wireshark 'Packet Comment' dialogs or packet details pane, not just typed into a text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: /home/ga/Documents/captures/http.cap. Output file: /home/ga/Documents/captures/http_annotated.pcapng. Total packets: 43. First HTTP GET request is packet 4. First HTTP 200 OK response is packet 27. Comment for packet 4 MUST be exactly 'Initial HTTP GET request to server'. Comment for packet 27 MUST be exactly 'Server responded with 200 OK'. The output must be in pcapng format to preserve comments.
